chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.object: atomic raw materials or purchased parts

entities_post_resolution.name: Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Batch is a production lot representing all units produced together in a single manufacturing run, with associated attributes such as production date, quantity, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches record... quality control status. | Batches undergo Quality Control inspections | Batches may have expiry dates for perishable goods | ## batch

Tracks production lots for traceability and inventory management. | | batch_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique batch identifier | | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product produced | | | production_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Production date | | | quantity_produced | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK > 0 | Quantity produced | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Storage location | | | expiry_date | DATE | | Expiry date (if applicable) | | | qc_status | qc_stat

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component is a discrete item used as a building block in manufacturing, such as atomic raw materials or purchased parts, procured from external suppliers.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | Components are the building blocks used in manufacturing and are procured from external suppliers. | Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many) | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Suppliers provide multiple Components (many-to-many via component_supplier) | # component

Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | - One-to-many with inventory | - Optional relationship with specification | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) |

entities_post_resolution.name: Inventory
entities_post_resolution.definition: The inventory entity represents current stock levels of components and finished goods stored in warehouses, including quantities on hand, quantities reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger replenishment.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Inventory represents the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses. | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date. | Inventory belongs to a Warehouse | Inventory tracks either a Component or Product | Work Orders consume Inventory (components) | Products are stored in Inventory records | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution. | - One-to-many with inventory | ## inventory

Tracks stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations. | | inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: Product is the manufactured item in the system (finished goods, assemblies, and components) with a unique ID, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time, and participates in BOMs and production processes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex products can contain simpler products as parts. | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Products are stored in Inventory records | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child) | Work Orders produce Products | # product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | - A product can contain other products as components via BOM | - Supports unlimited nesting depth for complex assemblies | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product refer

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: The QC status is the batch-level attribute indicating the current quality-control outcome (pending, passed, failed, or quarantined) used to drive workflow and release decisions.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_status | qc_status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, PASSED, FAILED, QUARANTINED | | Batches record... quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Completed
entities_post_resolution.definition: A numeric field quantity_completed (DECIMAL(10,2)) representing the number of items completed for an order; NOT NULL with DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 and <= ordered.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_completed | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0, <= ordered | Quantity completed |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Reserved
entities_post_resolution.definition: Reserved quantity is the portion of on-hand inventory that has been allocated or reserved for pending orders, reducing available stock for other use.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_reserved | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Reserved quantity | | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Specification
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Specification is a formal document or set of technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria used to define and verify materials, components, or products.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. | Specifications define requirements for Components | Specifications are used in Quality Control inspections | Specifications have version control for engineering changes | ## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products. | | specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier | | | specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name | | | version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version | | | effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date | | | spec_type | spec_type_t | NOT NULL | DIMENSIONAL, MATERIAL, PERFORMANCE | | | critical_parameter | VARCHAR(100) | | Parameter being tested | | | min_value | DECIMAL(10,3) | | Minimum acceptable value | | | max_value | DECIMAL(10,3) | | Maximum acceptable value | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | | Test unit of measure | | QC tests a

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity representing a party that provides components to the company, related to inbound shipments and having a many-to-many relationship with components.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Suppliers provide multiple Components (many-to-many via component_supplier) | Suppliers are linked to Inbound Shipments | Inbound Shipments reference Suppliers | **Relationships:**
- Many-to-many with supplier via component_supplier junction table | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many)

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Parent Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A uniqueness constraint on (component_id, supplier_id) that prevents creating duplicate supplier associations for the same component.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Constraints:
- `uq_bom_structure` UNIQUE (parent_product_id, component_product_id) — prevents duplicate entries | Constraints:
- `uq_component_supplier` UNIQUE (component_id, supplier_id) — prevents duplicate supplier associations

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Warehouse is a physical storage location in inventory management used to store materials, components, and finished goods, identified by a unique warehouse_id and named warehouse_name, and characterized by capacity and geographic location information.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. | Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution. | Inventory belongs to a Warehouse | | warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name | | Work Orders are associated with Warehouses | ## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse location | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Production location | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Storage location |

entities_post_resolution.name: Component Via Component Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A junction table that represents the association between components and their approved suppliers, including pricing and lead time information.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Relationships:**
- Many-to-many with supplier via component_supplier junction table | Junction table linking components to their approved suppliers with pricing and lead time information. | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique component identifier | | Indexes:
- `idx_cs_component` on component_id | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) | | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | | Self-Referential Structure:
- Both `parent_product_id` and `component_product_id` reference the product table | | component_supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique relationship identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Composition
entities_post_resolution.definition: The product composition is the set and hierarchical arrangement of components and sub-assemblies that comprise a parent product as specified by the Bill of Materials.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure.

entities_post_resolution.name: Idx Batch Warehouse
entities_post_resolution.definition: Index named idx_batch_date on the production_date column used to optimize queries filtering by batch production date.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Indexes:**
- `idx_batch_warehouse` on warehouse_id | **Indexes:**
- `idx_batch_date` on production_date | **Indexes:**
- `idx_batch_product` on product_id

entities_post_resolution.name: Idx Route Sequence
entities_post_resolution.definition: A database index on the route table spanning (product_id, sequence_number) to optimize lookups that filter by both product and sequence.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Indexes:**
- `idx_route_sequence` on (product_id, sequence_number) | **Indexes:**
- `idx_route_product` on product_id

entities_post_resolution.name: Parent Product Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: The unique identifier for the parent product in a bill of materials or hierarchical assembly, used as a foreign key referencing the product_id in the product table.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product for hierarchical assemblies | | **Indexes:**
- `idx_product_parent` on parent_product_id (WHERE NOT NULL) — supports hierarchical queries | **Self-Referential Relationship:**
- `parent_product_id` references `product_id` — enables multi-level product assemblies | | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) | | Indexes:
- `idx_bom_parent` on parent_product_id — BOM explosion queries | Self-Referential Structure:
- Both `parent_product_id` and `component_product_id` reference the product table | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | | | produ

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity On Hand
entities_post_resolution.definition: The quantity_on_hand is the available quantity of inventory currently in stock, i.e., stock on hand not allocated to pending orders.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity | | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Specification
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity representing a formal specification (a document or standard) identified by a unique specification_id and described by a specification_name.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. | Specifications define requirements for Components | Specifications are used in Quality Control inspections | Specifications have version control for engineering changes | ## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products. | | specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier | | | specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name | | | version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version | | | effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date | | | spec_type | spec_type_t | NOT NULL | DIMENSIONAL, MATERIAL, PERFORMANCE | | | critical_parameter | VARCHAR(100) | | Parameter being tested | | | min_value | DECIMAL(10,3) | | Minimum acceptable value | | | max_value | DECIMAL(10,3) | | Maximum acceptable value | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | | Test unit of measure | | QC tests a

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: External vendor entity used in procurement, identified by a unique supplier_id and described by supplier_name.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Stores external vendor information for component procurement. | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier | | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | | Indexes:
- `idx_cs_supplier` on supplier_id | | supplier_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Supplier name |

entities_post_resolution.name: City Location
entities_post_resolution.definition: The City location attribute representing the city associated with the record, stored as VARCHAR(50).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |

entities_post_resolution.name: In Progress Completed Closed
entities_post_resolution.definition: An enumerated set of lifecycle statuses for a generic workflow or task, from Pending through In Progress to Completed or Closed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | status | status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CLOSED | | | qc_type | qc_type_t | NOT NULL | INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, FINAL |

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipment: the logistical movement of goods between locations in a supply chain, including inbound transfers from suppliers to warehouses and outbound transfers to customers, tracked by type, source, destination, dates, and status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | Shipments track shipment type, source and destination locations, dates for ship, estimated arrival, and actual delivery, along with current status. | Inbound Shipments reference Suppliers | Outbound Shipments reference Customers | Shipments update Inventory levels | Shipments fulfill Work Order outputs | Suppliers are linked to Inbound Shipments | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Work Orders produce output for Shipment | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

entities_post_resolution.name: Type Of Inspection
entities_post_resolution.definition: The type of inspection refers to the category or kind of inspection recorded for QC events (e.g., incoming, in-process, final).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. | | qc_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Inspection date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Inspection Notes
entities_post_resolution.definition: A textual field in quality control/inspection records used to store remarks and supplementary information about an inspection, including type, date, inspector, results, and any notes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | notes | TEXT | | Inspection notes | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Last Restock Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The date (or timestamp) indicating the most recent restock event for a given item (component or product) in inventory records.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | last_restock_date | DATE | | Last restock timestamp | | Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date.

entities_post_resolution.name: Null Default
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quantity: a positive decimal value representing the required amount, with a default of 1.000 and a constraint that it must be greater than 0.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | | | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | | | product_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Product name | | | component_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Component name | | | supplier_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Supplier name | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) | | | product_type | product_type_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'FINISHED_GOOD' | Product classification: FINISHED_GOOD, ASSEMBLY, COMPONENT | | | is_active | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Y' | Active status flag | | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time | | | bom_level | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Depth in product hierarchy | | | quantity | DECIMAL(10,3) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1.000, CHECK > 0 | Required quantity |

entities_post_resolution.name: Defect Count
entities_post_resolution.definition: The numeric count of defects found during quality control inspections or testing.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | defect_count | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Number of defects found | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Work Order is a manufacturing instruction to plan and execute production of a specified quantity of a product, tracking its lifecycle from planned through completed and supporting hierarchical relationships such as a parent work order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition | | Shipments fulfill Work Order outputs | Products are used in Work Orders for production | A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Work Orders track planned versus completed quantities, status through the production lifecycle, priority levels, and planned dates. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies. | Work Orders produce Products | Work Orders are associated with Warehouses | Work Orders consume Inventory (components) | Work Orders produce output for Shipment | ## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support. | | work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Reorder Threshold
entities_post_resolution.definition: A numeric threshold for on-hand inventory that triggers a replenishment order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | reorder_threshold | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00 | Reorder trigger level | | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Route
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Route is the defined sequence of manufacturing operations, work centers, and timing that governs how a product moves through production.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Routes collectively define the manufacturing workflow and are used for production scheduling, capacity planning, and cost calculation. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number. | ## route

Defines manufacturing workflow steps and operations for product production. | | route_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique route identifier | | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product reference | | | route_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Route name | | | sequence_number | INT | NOT NULL, CHECK > 0 | Operation sequence | | | operation_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Operation description | | | work_center | VARCHAR(50) | | Work center location | | | cycle_time_minutes | DECIMAL(8,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Per-unit processing time | | | setup_time_minutes | DECIMAL(8,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Fixed set

entities_post_resolution.name: Physical Storage Location Information
entities_post_resolution.definition: A physical storage location is a tangible site used to store inventory such as materials, components, and finished goods (e.g., a warehouse).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | | capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK > 0 | Storage capacity | | A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored.

entities_post_resolution.name: Street Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A textual database field that stores the physical street address (house/building number, street, city, state/region, and postal code) of a person or entity.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Work Order Identifier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A primary key used to uniquely identify a quality-control (QC) record.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier | | ## quality_control

Stores inspection and testing records for quality assurance.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| qc_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique QC identifier | | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.

entities_post_resolution.name: Varchar
entities_post_resolution.definition: Variable-length character string data type in relational databases used to store text up to a specified maximum length.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Technical specification reference | | | component_category | VARCHAR(50) | | Component category classification |

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Job
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production job represents a manufacturing work order or run to produce a specified quantity of a product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | ## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support. | A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | ## batch

Tracks production lots for traceability and inventory management.

entities_post_resolution.name: Atomic Raw Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: A component representing basic production inputs consisting of atomic raw materials and purchased parts obtained from external suppliers.
synonyms: atomic raw materials or purchased parts
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
# component

Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.

entities_post_resolution.name: Standard Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: Base manufacturing cost per unit for producing a component.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | | | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost |

entities_post_resolution.name: Cycle Time Minutes
entities_post_resolution.definition: cycle_time_minutes is the time, in minutes, required to process a single unit (per-unit processing time).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | cycle_time_minutes | DECIMAL(8,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Per-unit processing time | | | setup_time_minutes | DECIMAL(8,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Fixed setup time |

entities_post_resolution.name: Depth In Hierarchy
entities_post_resolution.definition: The level of an item within the bill of materials (BOM) hierarchy, indicating its position in the hierarchical structure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: BOM level (depth in hierarchy) | bom_level tracks depth for performance optimization

entities_post_resolution.name: Effective Date

entities_post_resolution.name: Hierarchical Product Relationships
entities_post_resolution.definition: A data-model concept representing the hierarchical, parent-child relationships among product items (finished goods, assemblies, components) within a product structure, typically implemented via a self-referencing relationship.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
# product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies.

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufactured Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including their identity, name, category, hierarchical parent product relationships, cost, production or delivery lead time, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about individual components, including identification, descriptive details, category, measurement units, standard cost, and associated specification references.

tables_enriched.name: Bill of Materials
tables_enriched.description: Stores the composition of products, listing which component products make up each parent product and the required quantity and unit of measure for each component, including optional components and BOM... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about suppliers, including contact details, rating, and whether they are marked as preferred suppliers.

tables_enriched.name: Warehouse
tables_enriched.description: Stores data about warehouses, including their identifying information, location, capacity, and the manager responsible for each warehouse.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: Stores current inventory levels by product and warehouse, including on-hand quantity, reserved quantity, and the threshold to trigger restock, along with the date of the last restock.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: Stores data about work orders for products, including quantities, status, priority, planned scheduling, and related parent orders and warehouse.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments including their type, associated warehouse, supplier, and customer, along with scheduled and actual arrival dates and current status.

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of quality control checks performed on batches, including related specification and inspector information, results, counts of defects, and any notes.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: Stores specifications and their parameters, including versioning, effective date, type, and the allowed value range with units.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about production batches, including which product and warehouse they belong to, production and expiry dates, quantity produced, and current quality control status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: Stores the sequence of operations required to manufacture or assemble a product, including operation details, timing, and the work centers involved for each route step.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores relationships between components and suppliers, including whether a supplier is preferred and the lead time and price for obtaining a component from that supplier.
